description: Stage 3 backend reconciler for projects generated by agentNewSolution2. V1 is AUTONOMOUS and CREATE-ONLY: with no user prompt, it scans l4 for owners with statusBackend = toCreate, derives the 3-layer HEXAGONAL backend, generates the .defs.ts and materializes them to .ts, then marks the owners done. If there is nothing to create, it finishes immediately without writing files or statuses. Independent of statusFrontend (agentChangeFrontend). See spec.md in this folder.

entry.noInputPolicy: No user prompt. The agent receives no module/owner/item arguments; it discovers all actionable work from l4 statusBackend. (A single-item arg is an optional future convenience, not required.)
entry.noWorkBehavior: If no owner with statusBackend = toCreate is found, finish without changing any file or status.

source.schemas: _102021_/l2/agentChangeBackend/cbSchemas.ts (strict JSON schemas; collab-llm enforces the tool call, cbExtract re-validates) — TO BUILD

v1: Run autonomously with no user prompt; discover work from l4 statusBackend.
Process ONLY owners with statusBackend = toCreate (new). Exit cleanly when there is no create work.
Derive aggregates from ontology kind + relationships; apply the JSONB model (indexed columns out, the rest + child collections in details).
Generate the 3-layer hexagonal .defs.ts (domain, application, adapters) and materialize them to .ts via the reused L1 pipeline.
Register the persistence manifest, the http router and the port->adapter composition root; fix l0/config.json backend entrypoints.
Mark processed owners statusBackend = done after their artifacts + .ts validate.
Use JSON-schema-backed tool output whenever an LLM is used (agentNewSolution2 pattern).
explicitlyNotInV1: No user-provided module/owner/item filters (pure autonomous scan).
No clarification / no blocking human input.
No processing of toUpdate / toRemove / inProgress / done owners.
deferredToV2: toUpdate diff and preservation of manual edits.
toRemove cascade (deprecate table/impl, drop with confirmation, data safety).
Patching artifacts created by a previous run.
Critic/repair LLM loop for referential integrity beyond the deterministic checks.
notResponsible: horizontalOwned tables (owned by the horizontal module, e.g. Payment).
Pages / screens / menu, per-page contract authoring, frontend client functions (statusFrontend / agentChangeFrontend).
Authoring ontology / operations / workflows / rules (consumed only).

conventions.selfContained: Everything lives in l2/agentChangeBackend/. The only cross-folder reuse is the L1 materialization in l2/agentMaterializeSolution/.
conventions.agentNaming: Root + focused agents: agentCb* (clean single-purpose prompts). Materialization agents are REUSED by their existing names (agentPrepareDefsL1, agentMaterializeL1Def, agentMaterializeGen).
conventions.toolCallJsonSchema: Every LLM step returns its result through ONE tool call validated against a strict JSON schema (agentNewSolution2/ns2Schemas.ts pattern + the existing submitL1Pipeline tool). collab-llm enforces the schema; cbExtract re-validates before saving. No free-form prose results.
conventions.inputLayer: Reads l4 (BUSINESS, frozen by Stage 1): l4/{module}/module.defs.ts (ontology index + relationships), l4/{module}/ontology/*.defs.ts (canonical entity kind/fields), l4/operations/*.defs.ts, l4/workflows/*.defs.ts, l4/rules/*.defs.ts. The page bffCommands + per-page contract (l2/{module}/web/contracts/{page}.defs.ts) are CONSUMED when present (produced by agentChangeFrontend). Writes l1/{module} (3 hexagonal layers).
conventions.hexagonalLayers: layer_1_external/adapters (http: routes + controllers = BFF; persistence: repositories + TableDefinition; queues/webhooks/cron/plugins) ; layer_2_application (usecases/services/ports/dto/commands/queries) ; layer_3_domain (entities/value-objects/domain-services/rules/events). Dependency points INWARD; ctx.data ONLY inside adapters/persistence.
conventions.aggregateDerivation: From ontology kind + relationships: core -> 1 physical table + domain aggregate root + repository port + repository adapter ; supporting (oneToMany/oneToOne under a core, no direct query) -> EMBEDDED in the root details JSONB as a value-object (NO own table) ; event (append-only) -> own table ; mdm -> NO table (read-only via 102034 shared MDM runtime).
conventions.jsonbModel: Maximize JSONB. Real columns ONLY for fields that need an index (PK, queried FKs, status/lifecycle, ordering/filter timestamps used by operations/workflows). Everything else + supporting child collections -> single details JSONB column (e.g. Order.details.items: OrderItem[]). Promote a details field to a column only when it becomes filtered/indexed.
conventions.mdmPersistence: MDM table persistence is EXCLUSIVELY 102034 (mdm_documents, mdm_documents_entities_index, ...). This flow NEVER emits an MDM TableDefinition and NEVER registers an MDM table in the manifest; it only reads via the shared MDM runtime. horizontalOwned follows the same principle.
conventions.namingDeterminism: All code identifiers derive deterministically from the canonical ontology entityId / operationId (Order -> domain Order, table order/orders, port OrderRepository, usecase createOrder). The PT title ('Pedido') is a UI label ONLY — never a file/class/column name. (Fixes the legacy Order -> pedidoEntity drift.)

ownerStatus.failureStatus: leave as inProgress and write a trace record; v1 has no failed enum
ownerStatus.independentOf: statusFrontend (agentChangeFrontend) — no mandatory order between the two reconcilers

layerKnowledge.problem: The BFF must know the application (layer_2) functions; the application must know the domain; and persistence must serve the domain — but the DOMAIN must not know persistence. How is the chain wired?
layerKnowledge.compileTime: Resolved by the materialization dependsFiles (.d.ts) chain, generated INNER-FIRST: domain (imports nothing) -> repository port + table (import domain) -> usecase (imports domain + port) -> repository adapter (imports port + domain + table) -> http controller (imports the usecase function/types + the per-page contract Output). Each artifact's pipeline lists EXACTLY the inner-layer .d.ts it imports, so agentMaterializeGen always has the callee's signatures as context.
layerKnowledge.runtime: Dependency Inversion. The usecase depends ONLY on the repository PORT interface (layer_2_application/ports), NEVER on the concrete adapter. register-backend builds the composition root that binds each port -> its adapter; usecases resolve repositories from ctx by port (same registry pattern as ctx.data.moduleData.getTable today). So persistence depends on the domain/port — not the reverse.
layerKnowledge.correction: There is NO domain->persistence dependency. The arrow is INVERTED: persistence (adapter) implements a port owned by the application, and imports the domain; the domain is the innermost ring and imports nothing. BFF->application and application->domain are normal inward imports; application<->persistence is inverted through the port.

conventions.defsSelfSufficiency: Each generated .defs.ts MUST be SUFFICIENT to generate its .ts: agentMaterializeGen (a separate agent) consumes ONLY the .defs.ts, nothing else. So every .defs.ts carries (a) the complete `data` planning block (the WHAT) and (b) `export const pipeline` = one item with everything the LLM needs: outputPath (.ts), defPath, dependsFiles[] (.d.ts signatures of the inner callee layer), dependsOn[], skills[] = the LLM CONTEXT (layer skill _102021_/l2/skills/layer_*.md + platform _102034_.d.ts), rulesPath?+rulesApplied? (authoritative rule text), afterSaveBackEnd? (deterministic register hook), agent='agentMaterializeGen'. If the LLM would need anything not in `data` nor reachable via dependsFiles/skills/rulesPath, the .defs.ts is incomplete. Canonical example: _102043_/l1/cafeFlow/layer_1_external/dailySalesMetrics.defs.ts.
conventions.materialization: Reuses the L1 pipeline: gen-* writes the `data` block; agentPrepareDefsL1 appends `export const pipeline` to each .defs.ts in dependency order; agentMaterializeL1Def resolves outputPath + dependsFiles + attaches skills/rulesPath per item via tool call; agentMaterializeGen generates the .ts from the self-sufficient .defs.ts. Then deterministic registrars wire the manifest, the router and the composition root.
conventions.fanOutReads: Parallel fan-out children are pre-allocated, reused and DELETED after completion. Consumers that run AFTER a fan-out read the SAVED .defs.ts files (file-fallback), never task payloads.
conventions.verification: Deterministic, NON-blocking: index agents check id resolution against canonical l4 ids (unresolved -> warnings); the final validate reads the SAVED l1 files (ctx.data only in adapters/persistence; no MDM table; contract shape honored).
conventions.modelPolicy: No LLM for deterministic scan, status mutation, registration and validation. When an LLM is required, force strict JSON-schema tool output. Alias by task: codeawsfast (cheap/fast) for light steps; codehigh (strong reasoning, low cost) for planning (aggregates, JSONB/index, usecase orchestration, domain); codepro (mid) for structured defs + pipeline resolution; codeinstruct (priciest, best code) RESERVED for the final .ts generation (agentMaterializeGen).

modelAliases._note: Test aliases. Stored here so the flow is self-describing; each LLM step picks one via its `modelAlias` field. modelType hints inside prompts (e.g. <!-- modelType: codepro -->) must match the chosen alias.

recommendedFor: highest-risk code where correctness matters most
reasoning-heavy planning: aggregate boundaries, JSONB/index decisions

reads: l2/{module}/web/contracts/{page}.defs.ts (per-page contract, when frontend already ran)
writes: statusBackend on processed l4 operation/workflow owners
explicitlyNotWritten: horizontalOwned tables (owned by the horizontal module)
Pages / screens / menu / per-page contract / frontend client functions (statusFrontend)
ontology / operations / workflows / rules (consumed, never authored)

steps.goal: Root orchestrator. No input args. Start an autonomous scan for statusBackend = toCreate work and spawn the create chain. If the scan finds no work, finish immediately.
Deterministically read l4/l5 and select all owners (operations/workflows) with statusBackend = toCreate. toUpdate/toRemove/inProgress/done are reported but NOT processed in v1.
steps.output: A create change-set: moduleName, selected operations/workflows, current statuses, source file refs.
steps.ifEmpty: Complete the flow with no file/status writes.

steps.goal: Deterministic preflight on the selected owners only: entity/operation ids resolve to canonical l4 ids; ontology kind + relationships present; rules resolve; no selected owner already inProgress/done.
Deterministically set statusBackend = inProgress for the validated toCreate owners. The only status mutation before successful completion.
Derive aggregate boundaries from ontology kind + relationships. Emit aggregates[] {aggregateId, rootEntity, embeddedMembers[], events[], mdmRefs[]}. Deterministic ref check + checkpoint.

Apply the JSONB model per aggregate/event table: pick the few indexed columns (PK, queried FKs, status/lifecycle, ordering/filter timestamps); push everything else + child collections into details JSONB. Emit tables[] {tableId, ownership, indexedColumns[], detailsFields[], childCollections[]}. MDM produces NO table.
Map each pending operation/workflow to a usecase + the repository ports it needs (reads/writes -> aggregates). Emit usecases[] {usecaseId, operationId|workflowId, ports[], rulesApplied[]}.

steps.skipWhen: no page contract exists yet (frontend not run)
steps.goal: When page bffCommands + contracts exist, index the available page contracts for Output refinement. Route keys must reuse L4 bffName / contract routeKey when present; {module}.{page}.{command} is only a legacy fallback. Emit pages[] {pageId, commands[], contractRef}.
Per aggregate: the PURE domain entity (canonical camelCase fields, invariants, status/lifecycle) + embedded supporting members as value-objects. No persistence, no ctx.data. Name from entityId.
Per aggregate: the repository PORT interface I{Entity}Repository (getById, list(filter), save(aggregate), domain finders), typed in domain terms. layer_2_application/ports.

Per table (core root + event): the TableDefinition derived deterministically from the domain entity + persistence index — indexed columns out, details JSONB for the rest, snake_case. MDM/horizontal entities produce NO table.

Per aggregate: the repository ADAPTER implementing the port — maps domain <-> row (columns + details JSONB with child collections), resolves MDM reads via 102034, writes metrics. The ONLY place with ctx.data.

Per operation/workflow: the usecase (WHAT happens) orchestrating domain + ports, applying rulesApplied, wrapping multi-aggregate writes in one transaction. Imports ports + domain ONLY — never the concrete adapter, never ctx.data.

Per selected L4 owner: one BFF controller handler that calls the owner's usecase. Route key reuses owner.bffName; fallback is {module}.{pageId}.{commandName} only for L4 legacy. If a per-page frontend contract exists, use it only as Output refinement. No ctx.data, no persistence import.

REUSED. Append `export const pipeline` to every generated .defs.ts that lacks one, in dependency order (domain -> ports -> tables -> adapters -> usecases -> http), and spawn the per-file resolution fan-out. Ensures the singletons (router.ts, persistence.ts, module.ts, config.json).
REUSED. Per .defs.ts: resolve the pipeline item (outputPath .ts + dependsFiles .d.ts of the inner dependency layer) via tool call; deterministic layers (persistence table) skip the LLM.
REUSED. Per pipeline item: generate the final .ts from the .defs.ts + dependsFiles (.d.ts of the callee) + the layer skill. Most quality-sensitive step -> best code model (codeinstruct).

DETERMINISTIC (no LLM). Wire the persistence manifest (tableDefinitions), the http router (route entries) and the COMPOSITION ROOT binding each repository port -> its adapter; fix l0/config.json entrypoints to the hexagonal paths. Never registers MDM tables.

Barrier. Non-blocking coverage/integrity report read from the SAVED l1 files: every selected owner produced its artifacts + .ts; ctx.data only in adapters/persistence; no MDM/horizontal table emitted; every http handler matches its contract Output; ports bound in the composition root.
DETERMINISTIC. Set statusBackend = done for every selected owner whose artifacts + .ts validated. Leave unverified owners in inProgress with trace details (statusFrontend untouched).

AUTO-FINISH (no clarification): append the backend run to l5/{module}/process.defs.ts, concise report (aggregates, tables, usecases, controllers, owners done, warnings, v2-deferred), clear traces, clean task, complete. Summary viewable via openStepView.

failurePolicy.beforeLock: Fail without changing l4 owner statuses.
failurePolicy.afterLock: Do not mark done. Keep affected owners in inProgress and write trace details (no failed enum in v1).
failurePolicy.partialFailure: A failed artifact blocks done only for the owners that depend on it; unrelated owners may finish.
failurePolicy.noWork: No selected owners means no status or file writes.
failurePolicy.manualRecovery: A later recovery tool or v2 may reset stale inProgress owners to toCreate after inspection.

produces: l1/{module}/layer_2_application/* (usecases, services, ports, dto, commands, queries)
l1/{module}/layer_1_external/adapters/persistence/* (TableDefinition of core/event tables; repository adapters; manifest)
l1/{module}/layer_1_external/adapters/http/* (BFF controllers + routes; only when a contract exists)
statusBackend = done on the processed owners
explicitlyNotProduced: pages / screens / menu / contract authoring / frontend client functions (statusFrontend)
handoffArtifacts.independentOf: agentChangeFrontend (statusFrontend) — no mandatory order between the two reconcilers.
